page: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The instance provides 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the m4.2xlarge delivers moderate multi-core performance but exhibits limitations in single-core execution. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 25 percent, while multi-core performance reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, with read-only cached blocks reaching 351.67 GB/sec and uncached large blocks at 46.46 GB/sec. However, tasks like software compilation, image processing, and text processing score in the lower tier.

Given the lack of local storage and average processing capabilities, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as small-scale web serving, basic application hosting, and lightweight background processing. It is not optimized for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, or intensive LLM inference, as demonstrated by its poor performance on 7B parameter models. The hardware profile offers a balanced resource density for legacy applications that require stable, dedicated vCPUs without demanding modern high-frequency processor speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Xen hypervisor, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and data processing tasks generally fall into the lower tier. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including basic web serving, application hosting, and moderate multi-threaded background tasks that do not require high-speed local storage or intensive single-core compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, suitable for balanced workloads without local storage requirements.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 32 GB of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Xen hypervisor virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and no local storage
Best suited for general-purpose application hosting and web serving
